free_text: Investigation of 0+ states in 192Pt and 194Pt isotopes
Q3D spectrograf
Tandem accelerator of Maier Leibnitz
Laboratory of the Univ. and Tech. Univ. of Munich.
Targets of 194Pt and 196Pt with a thickness of 64 and
107 ug/cm2, respectively, deposited on 7.5 and
9 ug/cm2 carbon backing, respectively, were used.
The targets were 97.4% and 97.5% enriched in 194Pt
and 196Pt, respectively.
The most important impurities of the targets were
target enriched in 194Pt and 195Pt (1.5%), 194Pt
(0.6%), and 198Pt (0.29%) in the target enriched in
The outgoing tritons were analyzed with
the high-resolution Q3D magnetic spectrograph.
The particle identification and the position
determination were made with the 1-m-long focal plane
detector, which FWHM resolution of ~ 6 keV.
No information on source of uncertainties
Table I and II of Phys.Rev.C82(2010)024303
